content.problemStatement:
You are given an array A1A_1A1​, A2A_2A2​, . . ., ANA_NAN​ consisting of NNN integers.

A subarray AlA_lAl​, Al+1A_{l+1}Al+1​, Al+2A_{l+2}Al+2​, . . ., Ar−1A_{r−1}Ar−1​, ArA_rAr​ is good if the sum of elements of this subarray is equal to an integer KKK.

Find the number of good subarrays present in the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two integers NNN and KKK, NNN denoting the length of the array and KKK denoting the sum for a good subarray.
The second line of input will contain NNN integers A1A_1A1​, A2A_2A2​, . . . ANA_NAN​.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and K — the length of the array and the required sum. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of subarrays whose elements sum exactly to K.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Subarrays with sum 1 are the single elements A1 and A2, giving a total of 2 good subarrays.
The subarrays that sum to 5 are [A2, A3] (2+3) and [A3, A4] (3+2).
Each pair of consecutive 5s gives sum 10: (A1,A2), (A2,A3) and (A3,A4).

solution.approach: For each test case we compute prefix sums. A subarray [l, r] has sum K iff prefix[r] - prefix[l-1] = K. While scanning the array we keep a hash map that stores how many times each prefix sum has appeared so far. For the current prefix sum P we add to the answer the count of (P - K) already seen. Then we increment the count of P in the map. This runs in O(N) time and O(N) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for the hash map of prefix sums.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        K = int(next(it))
        pref = 0
        cnt = {0: 1}
        ans = 0
        for _ in range(N):
            pref += int(next(it))
            ans += cnt.get(pref - K, 0)
            cnt[pref] = cnt.get(pref, 0) + 1
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
